channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Popular Socialite Abu Trika Extradited to US for Alleged KSh 1bn Romance Scam
item.description: Frederick Kumi, known as Abu Trika, a prominent Ghanaian socialite, was extradited to the United States on July 9, 2026, for allegedly orchestrating a multi-million-dollar romance scam. The 31-year-ol...

item.title: Junior Springboks Focus on Balanced Approach Ahead of Semi-Final Clash with England
item.description: The Junior Springboks, coached by Kevin Foote, face England in the Junior World Championship semi-final in Tbilisi. The team's priority is to avoid a slow start, balance ball retention with kicking st...

item.title: Enugu Police Hunt for Bus Driver and Conductor Over Assault and Rifle Snatching
item.description: The Enugu State Police Command has started a manhunt for a bus driver and conductor after they allegedly assaulted police officers, engaged in dangerous driving, and snatched a police rifle. The incid...

item.title: Snowbirds Stage Final Hometown Show Before Temporary Hiatus Due to Jet Replacement
item.description: The Royal Canadian Air Force Snowbirds, Saskatchewan's iconic aerobatic team, performed their final hometown show in Moose Jaw on Saturday, marking the end of an era before a potential multi-year hiat...

item.title: 70 medical students return home after border closure relief
item.description: Around 70 medical students, who had been stranded at the Torkham border since October last year, finally returned to Pakistan after political intervention. Their academic sessions in Afghan institutio...

item.title: Wimbledon semi-finalist Arthur Fery meets royal family after historic run
item.description: Arthur Fery, a British Wimbledon semi-finalist, has met Prince George and Princess Charlotte during the men's singles final at the All England Club. The encounter, which followed Fery's remarkable ris...

item.title: PG&E Settles $22M Fine for Mosquito Fire Violations
item.description: Regulators in California are seeking $22 million from PG&E, alleging the utility provider committed violations linked to the 2022 Mosquito fire in Placer County. The fire, which ignited near the Oxbow...

item.title: Rising Costs Force Families to Cut Meat Use, Opt for Cheaper Proteins
item.description: In Abuja, rising living costs are pushing meat traders into tough times as families shift from buying beef to cheaper protein sources like fish, eggs, and cow skin (pomo). The Federal Capital Territor...

item.title: Cheryl Ladd's Role in the 1986 ABC Miniseries 'Crossings'
item.description: This article highlights Cheryl Ladd's portrayal of Liane DeVilliers in the 1986 ABC television miniseries 'Crossings,' an adaptation of Danielle Steel's bestselling World War II romance novel. The pie...

item.title: EFCC Appeals Defamation Ruling Against Former Minister
item.description: The Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC) has launched an appeal against a court ruling that awarded former Minister of Power and Steel, Olu Agunloye, N10 million in damages for defamation. ...

item.title: Increased Tornado Activity in Alberta and Saskatchewan Amid Extreme Weather
item.description: The Canadian Prairies experienced heightened tornado activity on Saturday night as a tornado touched down near the Alberta-Saskatchewan border. Environment Canada issued a red tornado warning for the ...

item.title: Armed Robber Attacks Salon Worker in Ruaka, Seizes Phone Worth Over KSh 100k
item.description: CCTV footage captured a violent robbery at a salon in Ruaka where an armed man assaulted a female employee and stole her phone valued at over KSh 100,000. The incident occurred on Friday, July 10, aft...

item.title: Funeral prayers held for K2 Airways engineer after cargo plane crash, amid calls for government action
item.description: Funeral prayers in absentia were held in Karachi for Muhammad Arif Siddiqui, a flight engineer who died in a K2 Airways cargo plane crash in the Arabian Sea. The Boeing 737-400 aircraft, en route from...

item.title: Senate Makes Impeachment Trial Documents Public for Transparency
item.description: The Senate has initiated the publication of documents and evidence related to the impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte on its official website, as announced by Senate President Sherwin Gat...

item.title: Re-polling results: PPP and IPP candidates retain seats in Gilgit-Baltistan
item.description: In Gilgit-Baltistan, re-polling results have confirmed the retention of seats by PPP candidate Mohammad Naseem and IPP candidate Mohammad Dilpazeer. After legal challenges led to the suspension of ini...

item.title: Florida man arrested after parking dispute escalates to gunfire
item.description: A 74-year-old man in Florida was arrested after a dispute over a parking space at a Publix grocery store escalated into a confrontation involving gunfire. According to law enforcement, the incident oc...

item.title: Key Details Revealed About the 2030 Men's FIFA World Cup
item.description: The 2030 FIFA Men's World Cup is set to be one of the most unique tournaments in history, spanning three continents with six host nations. Uruguay, Argentina, Paraguay, Spain, Portugal, and Morocco wi...

item.title: Murang'a Governor's Driver Shot During Attack in Nyahururu
item.description: Murang'a Governor Irungu Kangata's convoy was attacked by rowdy youths in Nyahururu, resulting in his driver being shot in the chest. The incident occurred during a public gathering ahead of the Ol-Ka...

item.title: America's 250th Anniversary Lacks Spectacular Celebrations
item.description: The article critiques the lackluster public celebrations marking America's 250th anniversary, contrasting it with past events like the bicentennial and centennials in other countries. Author Raymond J...

item.title: Tension between Tuchel and Bellingham threatens England's World Cup campaign
item.description: The article discusses the growing tension between England's head coach Thomas Tuchel and star player Jude Bellingham, which could jeopardise the team's World Cup prospects. Tuchel's critical comments ...
